artPrompt: A characterful portrait of a small unimpressive dragon hovering on lazy wings above the feral town square, clutching a teetering hoard of round wall thermostats and dials instead of gold, its expression blandly indifferent as sparks and confetti swirl past. Bonfire glow lights its dull scales from below, embers drifting. Mono-red palette of flame orange, ember red, and black smoke, deadpan whimsy amid chaos, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Room-Temperature Dragon
subtitle: Surprisingly Lukewarm
text:
Flying.
Room-Temperature Dragon's toughness equals the room's current temperature in degrees Celsius, rounded to whatever feels right.
Whenever it attacks, it deals fire damage equal to how many degrees above 20°C the room is. If the room is below 20°C, it deals cold damage and shivers instead, taking that much damage itself.
If anyone opens a window, this loses half a counter of dignity.

extras.impossibleMath: toughness from degrees, half a counter of dignity
